name: Third Secret of Fatima
description: The Third Secret of Fatima is the last of three secrets that the Virgin Mary purportedly revealed to three Portuguese children, Lúcia Santos and her cousins Jacinta and Francisco Marto, in 1917. The first two secrets involved visions of hell and a prophecy about the end of World War I and the rise and fall of communism. The Third Secret, however, was kept confidential by the Vatican until 2000, fueling speculation and conspiracy theories. Officially, the Vatican released an interpretation stating that the secret was a prophetic vision of the 20th-century persecution of Christians, culminating in an assassination attempt on Pope John Paul II in 1981. Proponents of alternative interpretations believe the released text is incomplete or deliberately misleading, arguing that the true secret foretells of greater apostasy within the Catholic Church, a global catastrophe, or the end times. Their arguments often cite perceived inconsistencies in the Vatican's explanation, the long delay in its release, and statements from individuals claiming to have seen the original text. Mainstream Catholic scholars generally accept the Vatican's interpretation, viewing the Third Secret as a warning against the dangers of the 20th century and a testament to the power of prayer and faith. They point to the historical context of the Cold War and the persecution of Christians in communist countries as evidence supporting the official interpretation. However, some within the Catholic Church continue to debate the completeness and accuracy of the released text.
subtitle: A Prophecy, Controversy, and Unfulfilled Expectations

claims.claim: The released Third Secret is incomplete or a fabrication.
claims.evidence: Perceived inconsistencies in the Vatican's explanation, delays in releasing the secret, claims from individuals who claim to have seen the original text and say it is different, the wording of the original letter.
claims.criticism: The Vatican maintains the released text is the complete Third Secret. Experts in textual analysis and cryptography have examined the document and found no evidence of significant omissions or fabrications. Differences in accounts are attributed to memory lapses and interpretations.

claims.claim: The Third Secret predicts a great apostasy within the Catholic Church.
claims.evidence: Interpretation of the released text as referring to a crisis of faith and moral decline within the Church, fueled by scandals and dissenting opinions. Some see it as a reference to the supposed infiltration of the Church by Freemasons or other groups.
claims.criticism: Mainstream Catholic theologians argue the secret refers to the general persecution of Christians and the importance of remaining faithful in the face of adversity. The concept of "apostasy" is interpreted broadly, not necessarily as a specific internal crisis.

claims.claim: The Third Secret foretells a global catastrophe or the end times.
claims.evidence: Interpretations of the vision of the 'bishop dressed in white' being killed as a symbol of the Pope's death and the destruction of the Church. Some connect it to other apocalyptic prophecies in the Bible and other religious traditions.
claims.criticism: The Vatican interprets the 'bishop dressed in white' being killed as a symbolic representation of the suffering of Christians in the 20th century, particularly the assassination attempt on Pope John Paul II. It is not necessarily a literal prophecy of the end of the world.

keyFigures.name: Lúcia Santos
keyFigures.role: One of the three visionaries who received the secret.

keyFigures.name: Pope John Paul II
keyFigures.role: The Pope during the release of the Third Secret and whose assassination attempt was linked to it.

keyFigures.name: Cardinal Joseph Ratzinger (Pope Benedict XVI)
keyFigures.role: Prefect of the Congregation for the Doctrine of the Faith who oversaw the release of the Third Secret and provided the official interpretation.

mythologyConnections: Virgin Mary (role as intercessor and protector)

timeline.event: The Virgin Mary appears to the three children in Fatima and reveals the three secrets.
Lúcia Santos writes down the first two secrets and the Third Secret, under obedience from the Bishop of Leiria.
The Third Secret is delivered to the Vatican.
The Vatican releases the official interpretation of the Third Secret.
Debate and speculation about the Third Secret continues.

sources.author: Congregation for the Doctrine of the Faith
sources.title: Congregation for the Doctrine of the Faith

variants: Our Lady of Fatima Third Secret
